wasserstein_vs_cka.interpretation: Negative rho means high Wasserstein (dissimilar distributions) corresponds to low CKA (dissimilar representations) — agreement. Near-zero rho means the two metrics capture different aspects.

alpha_vs_wasserstein.interpretation: Positive rho means high-alpha (steep spectrum, CKA-type) regions have larger Wasserstein distances across sessions — less distributional stability. Negative rho means CKA-type regions are more OT-stable.

wasserstein_vs_procrustes.interpretation: Positive rho means Wasserstein and UMAP Procrustes agree on which session pairs are more dissimilar. High correlation suggests both capture similar distributional structure.
